title: Gentlemans Intermission

jokes.text: So she can ride the maid! It is adorable.
jokes.setup: Showing baby item from Hermes, expensive luxury store
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions baby riding 'the maid' as if it's normal wealthy behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - only wealthy Avery would casually reference baby riding servants. Shows class disconnect with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Lemon went to middle school with a Charlotte who used to rub herself against a tree during recess.
jokes.setup: Discussing baby name Charlotte
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual detail about middle schooler
jokes.explanation: Shocking specificity creates humor through inappropriate oversharing. Well-structured with good economy.

jokes.text: Drive, intelligence, humility, chaos... or the acronym 'dihc.' I'm looking for dihc, Avery. And I'm gonna take it wherever I can find it.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining mentorship qualities with serious business tone
jokes.punchline: Acronym spells out inappropriate word, delivered with complete obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Masterful wordplay - takes serious business concept and creates accidental innuendo. Jack's obliviousness makes it perfect character comedy. Highly quotable and rewatchable.

jokes.text: Maybe you and Liz can raise it.
jokes.setup: Discussing Jack's mentoring relationship with Liz
jokes.punchline: Suggesting they raise the baby together like a couple
jokes.explanation: Absurd jealousy taken to logical extreme. Good economy but somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Crazy hormone swing.
jokes.setup: Just made completely inappropriate suggestion about Jack and Liz
jokes.punchline: Dismisses it as simple hormone issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. Economy is excellent - two words deflate the entire uncomfortable moment.

jokes.text: Why does everything smell like onions?
jokes.setup: Pregnancy affecting senses
jokes.punchline: Specific complaint about onion smell
jokes.explanation: Standard pregnancy symptom joke, functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Lemon. Boundaries.
jokes.setup: Previous conversation about boundaries with employee
jokes.punchline: Immediate practical application when Liz enters
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and immediate callback. Two words capture the entire dynamic shift.

jokes.text: They're just updating Tracy's to include his recent submarine D.U.I.
jokes.setup: Explaining why NBC is updating Tracy's obituary
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of impossible crime - submarine DUI
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd and specific to Tracy's character. The impossibility of the crime makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Tom was a fellow from my town we accidentally buried alive.
jokes.setup: Kenneth comparing Tracy to Tom Sawyer
jokes.punchline: Reveals dark truth about his 'Tom Sawyer' reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth - takes innocent reference and reveals disturbing backstory with complete sincerity. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Funny story, after we dug him back up, he tried to kill all of us.
jokes.setup: Just revealed they buried someone alive
jokes.punchline: Calls attempted murder a 'funny story'
jokes.explanation: Great follow-up that escalates the darkness while maintaining Kenneth's innocent tone.

jokes.text: Telephone etiquette is important. It lets people know your race even when they can't see you.
jokes.setup: Dick explaining why he says his name on phone calls
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific and inappropriate reasoning
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and specific reasoning that reveals character's odd worldview. Good setup/punchline structure.

jokes.text: Not really, I was gonna take this class called 'cooking for one,' but the teacher killed himself.
jokes.setup: Dad asking if she has a busy week
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of teacher's suicide in cooking class context
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually. The irony of a 'cooking for one' teacher committing suicide adds layers. Perfect Liz Lemon pathetic-life humor.

jokes.text: With you and mom here, the doormen will have to eat their words about me never having friends over.
jokes.setup: Liz excited about parents visiting
jokes.punchline: Reveals she has to use parents to prove she has friends
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz patheticness - even doormen judge her social life. Good character work.

jokes.text: Can't a guy have a little fun? - What is wrong with you? - What's wrong with you?
jokes.setup: Dick defensively asking about having fun
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal when mom gets on line
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing Dick's deception. Quick reversal works well.

jokes.text: Tracy Jordan, star of the fat bitch movies, was also voted 'worst representation of a black man' nine years in a row.
jokes.setup: Reading Tracy's obituary
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous movie titles and absurd award streak
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy characterization through fake career details. 'Nine years in a row' adds absurd specificity.

jokes.text: And thank God I didn't anybody, and thank God I my. And giving the queen parvo.
jokes.setup: Tracy's FCC fines being read
jokes.punchline: Censored speech that makes no sense plus impossible disease transmission
jokes.explanation: Clever way to show censored content while making it absurd. Queen getting parvo is random and funny.

jokes.text: That's how my grandkids will remember me as they fly around in their jet-packs?
jokes.setup: Tracy upset about his legacy
jokes.punchline: Casual assumption about future technology
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist touch - Tracy naturally assumes jet-packs will exist. Shows his odd perspective on time.

jokes.text: Who, Mark Cuban? That guy ran me over with a jet ski.
jokes.setup: Being called an idiot millionaire
jokes.punchline: Random celebrity grudge with impossible vehicle
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy non-sequitur. Random celebrity beef with impossible logistics.

jokes.text: Typical liberal media.
jokes.setup: Learning about pre-made obituaries
jokes.punchline: Jenna using political talking point inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment with Jenna parroting talking points, but somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: If they used any of the footage from my shoplifting arrest, that would be great because my arms looked fantastic.
jokes.setup: Jenna wanting to see her obituary
jokes.punchline: Hopes they use arrest footage because she looked good
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna - completely misses the point of obituary concern. Only cares about appearance even in arrest footage.

jokes.text: It must be my 'sesuality', because I am so very 'sesual'. You like that?
jokes.setup: Trying to explain why Avery feels threatened
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'sesuality' delivered with false confidence
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz trying to be sexy and failing. The made-up word and asking for approval makes it cringier.

jokes.text: She's put on considerable boob weight. She's not used to that, and sometimes she... just falls over.
jokes.setup: Explaining Avery's emotional state
jokes.punchline: Bizarre physics problem from pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Absurd take on pregnancy weight gain. 'Just falls over' is a funny visual.

jokes.text: Blammo. Solved it.
jokes.setup: Liz saying she'll solve her own problems
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives up with nonsense word
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz - immediate capitulation with made-up confident word. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: I played arts and literature in the film adaptation of Trivial Pursuit.
jokes.setup: Jenna arguing she deserves an obituary
jokes.punchline: Credits impossible movie adaptation of board game
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd fake credit. The specificity of playing a board game category in a movie adaptation is perfect.

jokes.text: Kim Jong-il? I never heard of her.
jokes.setup: Looking at obituary list
jokes.punchline: Doesn't know major world dictator, gets gender wrong
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna ignorance. Shows her complete disconnect from world events.

jokes.text: My pinky. - No, sir. - Then you wouldn't be perfect anymore.
jokes.setup: Jack testing Jonathan's ambition
jokes.punchline: Bizarre reasoning about perfection and finger
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jonathan's obsession with Jack. Absurd test works.

jokes.text: No, watch! I'll cut off my pinky and tell him it's yours.
jokes.setup: Jack saying Jonathan lacks ambition
jokes.punchline: Immediately offers to mutilate himself to help Jack
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonathan escalation. Shows his devotion through ridiculous means.

jokes.text: Ambition is the willingness to kill the things you love and eat them in order to stay alive. Haven't you ever read my throw pillow?
jokes.setup: Jack explaining ambition philosophy
jokes.punchline: Reveals this dark wisdom is on home decor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack - turning dark business philosophy into casual home decor reference. Great character integration.

jokes.text: A very hip Latino-sounding blur at Mervyn's said I looked good in it.
jokes.setup: Defending his shirt choice
jokes.punchline: Admits he couldn't see salesperson clearly due to no glasses
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Dick's vanity despite practical limitations. 'Blur' is a funny detail.

jokes.text: you show up here looking like you've been on spring break for 300 years
jokes.setup: Liz confronting her dad's behavior
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated aging metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good exaggeration. '300 years' adds absurd specificity to spring break reference.

jokes.text: Your 'gentleman's intermission.'
jokes.setup: Dick's euphemistic term for his time away
jokes.punchline: Liz sarcastically repeating the ridiculous phrase
jokes.explanation: Good callback to episode title. Shows how ridiculous the euphemism sounds.

jokes.text: Tonight, we're doing a push-up, and tomorrow, we're going to a bar called 'Swingles.'
jokes.setup: Dick bragging about plans with army buddy
jokes.punchline: Singular push-up reveals their actual physical limitations
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of machismo. 'A push-up' (singular) perfectly undercuts the tough guy act.

jokes.text: Gary's wife just died a couple of days ago, and he hasn't returned the hospital bed, so I can sleep there!
jokes.setup: Dick explaining sleeping arrangements
jokes.punchline: Inappropriately practical about recent death
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny - shows how pathetic their 'wild night' really is. Good character obliviousness.

jokes.text: Now, where's the door? - I'm not telling! - Cold. You need your glasses. Colder.
jokes.setup: Dick dramatically trying to leave
jokes.punchline: Can't find door without glasses, Liz gives directions like game
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup. Shows consequences of his vanity about glasses.

jokes.text: Sure, I Google myself all the time. Like when Angie's not in the mood, or I'm alone in the hotel.
jokes.setup: Asked if he Googles himself
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstands what Googling means
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection joke. Tracy's innocent misunderstanding creates double meaning. Excellent structure and very quotable.

jokes.text: You do know that Googling yourself means looking yourself up on the Internet. - I did not know that. That explains why Liz Lemon was so cool the other day.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining what Googling actually means
jokes.punchline: Tracy realizes his previous misunderstanding with Liz
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that retroactively makes an earlier scene funnier. Perfect structure for the revelation.

jokes.text: Liz Lemon, you mind if I Google myself in your office? - Sure. - Can I use your computer? - How else are you gonna do it?
jokes.setup: Flashback to Tracy's request
jokes.punchline: Shows Liz's innocent response to what she thought was innocent request
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure showing both characters' perspectives. Liz's innocent 'How else are you gonna do it?' is now hilariously inappropriate in hindsight.

jokes.text: When I go to sleep, nothing happens in the world.
jokes.setup: Tracy declaring independence from needing help
jokes.punchline: Reveals his completely warped sense of self-importance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy megalomania in just eight words. Shows his belief that reality revolves around him. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Maybe they'd stop calling him a 'bald wang' in front of the babysitter.
jokes.setup: Talking about respecting fathers
jokes.punchline: Specific inappropriate insult in wrong context
jokes.explanation: Funny specific insult. 'Bald wang' is a ridiculous phrase.

jokes.text: With guys like that, I always just say, 'who's younger... me or your daughter?' That usually creeps them out enough that they leave.
jokes.setup: Discussing old men at clubs
jokes.punchline: Brutal psychological warfare delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor. Shows Cerie's tactical approach to unwanted attention. Well-structured with good payoff.

jokes.text: Not me.
jokes.setup: Cerie's creepy line about ages
jokes.punchline: Liz's immediate uncomfortable response to thinking about her dad
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words convey Liz's horror at the implication about her father. Good reaction beat.

jokes.text: he owns the world's only giraffe basketball team, the New York necks.
jokes.setup: Listing Tracy's accomplishments
jokes.punchline: Impossible sports team with perfect pun name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist concept with perfect team name pun. 'New York Necks' is inspired wordplay.

jokes.text: Oh, really? Well, so are you!
jokes.setup: Jack saying she failed test of intelligence
jokes.punchline: Childish comeback that proves his point
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of Jenna's lack of intelligence through playground logic.

jokes.text: Like the song, I am taking on my business.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to sound businesslike
jokes.punchline: Butchers song lyric 'Takin' Care of Business'
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth malapropism. Gets song wrong in endearing way.

jokes.text: You're not welcoming people to castle Frankenstein.
jokes.setup: Telling Liz to fix her posture
jokes.punchline: Specific monster movie reference for bad posture
jokes.explanation: Good specific reference. Jack's precision in insults is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: We work for the FBI. Female Body Inspectors.
jokes.setup: Young woman asking what they do
jokes.punchline: Elderly man uses juvenile pickup line
jokes.explanation: Shows Dick's pathetic attempts at being cool. The line is intentionally bad/dated which works for character but isn't clever writing.

jokes.text: Because that ass is... - My father worked at Philadelphia power and light.
jokes.setup: Dick starting inappropriate pickup line
jokes.punchline: Gets interrupted, gives innocent family history instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure. The innocent response creates funny contrast with attempted pickup.

jokes.text: This woman's trying to hit on her own father!
jokes.setup: Liz disguised, talking to her dad
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding creates worst possible interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Shows how Liz's plan backfired spectacularly.

jokes.text: Let's go hit that bar with the cool rainbow flags.
jokes.setup: Dick and Gary leaving after embarrassment
jokes.punchline: Completely misunderstanding what rainbow flags signify
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dick's cluelessness about gay culture.

jokes.text: and three-time Tony... Shaloub sex partner
jokes.setup: Jenna making fake obituary with TV credits
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual boast mixed in with awards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna - mixing professional credits with inappropriate personal ones. Good absurdist touch.

jokes.text: Jenna Maroney, dead at... 32.
jokes.setup: Making her own obituary
jokes.punchline: Obviously false age claim
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna vanity. Lies about age even in fake death notice.

jokes.text: Then you said, 'read back what we have so far.'
jokes.setup: Kenneth reading back Tracy's notes
jokes.punchline: Literally writes down the instruction to read back notes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth literal-mindedness. Shows his inability to distinguish between content and instruction.

jokes.text: If I save it, I'm a double hero.
jokes.setup: Tracy seeing hero cat on TV
jokes.punchline: Bizarre logic that saving a hero makes you doubly heroic
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy logic - mathematical approach to heroism. Shows his childlike reasoning.

jokes.text: Let him hold a miniature golf scoring pencil to make them look normal, obviously.
jokes.setup: Telling someone about John Boehner's small hands
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific solution delivered as obvious fact
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack - highly specific practical solution delivered with complete confidence. 'Obviously' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I would never say this to her face, but Lemon is above average.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining difficulty finding mentee
jokes.punchline: Treats 'above average' as high praise too sensitive to say directly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack - his version of a glowing review is 'above average.' Shows his impossibly high standards and emotional constipation. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I'm not interested in this position unless I'm gonna be constantly praised. And I won't cut my hair.
jokes.setup: Jack interviewing potential mentee
jokes.punchline: Millennial stereotype demands with random addition
jokes.explanation: Fairly predictable millennial joke. Hair demand adds slight randomness but overall feels formulaic.

jokes.text: My obituary will read 'Oscar winner' instead of 'children's soccer heckler'.
jokes.setup: Tracy realizing Oscar would change his reputation
jokes.punchline: Reveals embarrassing specific current reputation
jokes.explanation: Great specific detail. 'Children's soccer heckler' is perfectly absurd and character-appropriate for Tracy.

jokes.text: I left Tracy Jr. in Atlantic City!
jokes.setup: Tracy feeling like he's forgetting something important
jokes.punchline: Casually abandoned his son in gambling city
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy parenting. The casual delivery and inappropriate location make it funny.

jokes.text: Die, hero cat! I hate you! I said I'm gonna kill that cat! Killing cats is wrong! Unless it's to make a hat! My skull!
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to attack the hero cat
jokes.punchline: Contradictory moral stance plus injury
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation. The hat exception is random enough to work but overall feels chaotic.

jokes.text: That makes me a double hero! Try to deny me my obit now, you jags!
jokes.setup: Jenna accidentally saving the cat Tracy was attacking
jokes.punchline: Applies Tracy's flawed double-hero logic plus dated insult
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Tracy's logic. 'Jags' is an oddly dated insult that fits character.

jokes.text: I'm wearing a Duane reade bag as underwear today.
jokes.setup: Proving she's not ready to move on from Jack's guidance
jokes.punchline: Deeply inappropriate personal detail proving dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz patheticness. Specific enough to be believable, gross enough to be funny. Great character integration.

jokes.text: I'm a mighty great white shark, Lemon, and you are a remora, clinging to me with your suction cup head.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining their relationship
jokes.punchline: Elaborate marine biology metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack - overwrought metaphor delivered with complete seriousness. 'Suction cup head' is a great detail.

jokes.text: eat my parasites.
jokes.setup: Finishing Jack's remora metaphor
jokes.punchline: Liz accepts gross role in relationship
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows Liz accepting the degrading metaphor. Perfect economy and character work.

jokes.text: I'm gonna find you, I'm gonna kick your ass so hard, you're gonna be throwing up my toenails.
jokes.setup: Jack pretending to be angry boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Impossible threat with gross specificity
jokes.explanation: Great absurd threat. The impossible physics of 'throwing up toenails' makes it memorable.

jokes.text: I'm a corrupt cop! My brother's a corrupt fireman. He's gonna set you on fire, and I am not gonna investigate it.
jokes.setup: Jack escalating the threatening character
jokes.punchline: Elaborate corruption scheme for petty revenge
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with internal logic. The bureaucratic corruption angle is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Actually, I don't have grandchildren. - Tricia told me you have a daughter. What's wrong with her?
jokes.setup: Jack questioning why Dick has no grandchildren
jokes.punchline: Immediately assumes something's wrong with the daughter
jokes.explanation: Good logical assumption that accidentally hits too close to home about Liz's life.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Daphne Donaghy I saw a turtle!
jokes.setup: Jack imagining future daughter
jokes.punchline: Random childish excitement about turtle
jokes.explanation: Shows Jack's awkwardness with children through random kid impression. Turtle detail adds specificity.

jokes.text: She can strip her way through community college. Come on.
jokes.setup: Jack considering name Kylie
jokes.punchline: Immediate assumption about life trajectory based on name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack classism and name prejudice. Dark but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: And every Tina I've known is a real judgmental bitch.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining why not to use Christina/Tina
jokes.punchline: Makes judgmental statement while calling others judgmental
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Jack being judgmental while criticizing judgment. Self-aware character moment.

tmdb_overview: Avery Jessup becomes suspicious of the relationship her fiancé Jack Donaghy has with his employee and friend Liz Lemon, so she orders Jack to set boundaries in their friendship. This proves a problem for Liz when her father, Dick Lemon arrives in town on a break from his marriage. Meanwhile, actor Tracy Jordan is upset when he discovers that his pre-recorded NBC obituary shows him to have done nothing worthwhile in his life, whilst Jenna Maroney learns of the video obituaries, she decides to make a video of her own.
insight: Seventy jokes in 22 minutes: 30 Rock's densest episode yet, fueled by absurdist chaos.
